original_hypothesis.title: Bribe Reward Claims Permanently DoS'd by Unbounded earned() Loop with WEEK=1800
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The Bribe.earned() function iterates over every epoch since lastEarn with no iteration cap. With WEEK=1800 seconds (30 minutes), epochs accumulate at 48/day. After ~8-10 real-world days without claiming, earned() requires 384-480 iterations, each performing two binary searches over storage (getPriorBalanceIndex + getPriorSupplyIndex). At ~39,000 gas per iteration, this exceeds Avalanche's 15M block gas limit. Since lastEarn is only updated inside getReward() which calls earned(), the DoS is self-reinforcing and permanent.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The combination of WEEK=1800 (extremely short epochs for production) and unbounded iteration creates a practical gas DoS after just days of inactivity. This affects any user who doesn't claim every few days.

proof_of_signal.pattern: for (uint256 i = 0; i < numEpochs; i++) loop with no upper bound; numEpochs = (epochStart(now) - lastEarnEpoch) / WEEK; WEEK=1800

preconditions: User hasn't claimed bribe rewards for ~8-10 days

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: earned() loop count = (current_epoch - last_earn_epoch) / 1800; after 10 days = 480 iterations * ~39K gas/iter ≈ 18.7M gas > 15M limit
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin function can reset lastEarn or paginate claims
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Grows worse over time — once DoS'd, can never be un-DoS'd

operational_questions: What is the actual gas cost per iteration on Avalanche?
How many active bribe tokens exist (loop in getReward is per-token)?
Is there any mechanism to partially claim or advance lastEarn?
validation_requirements: Gas profile earned() with increasing epoch gaps
Confirm no alternative claim path exists
Test with realistic checkpoint counts for binary search depth

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: User votes and earns bribe rewards (normal usage)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: User doesn't claim for 8-10 days (passive — vacation, forgotten wallet, etc.)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: getReward call reverts due to out-of-gas in earned() loop
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Rewards are permanently locked — lastEarn never updates, loop only grows longer

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: All bribe rewards permanently locked for affected users
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Any user inactive for >8 days loses all accumulated bribe rewards forever
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Impossible without contract upgrade; no admin recovery path
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Bribe reward distribution for all long-term holders

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: None — affects normal users through inactivity

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Users unable to claim bribe rewards; revert traces showing out-of-gas
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Growing bribe token balances in Bribe contracts with no claims
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Sudden inability to claim after period of inactivity

validation_result.mechanism: unbounded epoch loop with short WEEK causing gas DoS
validation_result.impact_summary: Bribe rewards become permanently unclaimable for any user who doesn't claim within ~8-10 days, due to the earned() loop exceeding block gas limits with WEEK=1800s. The DoS is self-reinforcing since lastEarn is only updated via getReward() which calls earned().
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The Bribe.earned() function iterates over every epoch since the last claim (lastEarn) with no upper bound on iterations. With WEEK=1800 seconds (30 minutes) hardcoded in BlackTimeLibrary, epochs accumulate at 48 per day. After ~8-10 days without claiming, earned() requires 384-480+ iterations. Each iteration performs two binary searches over storage (getPriorBalanceIndex + getPriorSupplyIndex), each involving multiple cold SLOADs at 2100 gas each. This quickly exceeds Avalanche's ~15M block gas limit. Since lastEarn is only updated inside getReward() which calls earned() first, once the DoS threshold is crossed, it becomes permanent — the loop only grows longer over time with no escape hatch.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: BlackTimeLibrary.sol line 4 confirms WEEK=1800. Bribes.sol lines 85-107 show the unbounded loop in earned(). Bribes.sol lines 224-235 show getReward() calls earned() then updates lastEarn — if earned() reverts, lastEarn never advances. No alternative claim path or pagination mechanism exists in the contract.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The loop iterates numEpochs times without any cap. numEpochs = (epochStart(now) - lastEarnEpoch) / 1800. After 10 days, numEpochs ≈ 480. Each iteration performs two binary searches with cold SLOADs (~30-60K gas per iteration), totaling ~15-29M gas which exceeds block gas limits.
getReward() is the ONLY function that updates lastEarn. It calls earned() first — if earned() reverts due to OOG, lastEarn is never updated, making the DoS permanent and self-reinforcing.

WEEK is hardcoded as 1800 seconds (30 minutes). Standard ve(3,3) implementations use 604800 (7 days). The 336x shorter epoch duration means the earned() loop accumulates iterations 336x faster than comparable protocols.

calling_sequence: 1. gaugeManager calls Bribe.getReward(tokenId, tokens) on behalf of user
2. getReward iterates over each token, calling earned(tokenId, tokens[i])
4. earned() enters unbounded for loop: for (uint256 i = 0; i < numEpochs; i++)
5. Each iteration calls getPriorBalanceIndex (binary search over storage) and getPriorSupplyIndex (binary search over storage)
6. After ~428-480 iterations (~8.9-10 days), gas exceeds 15M block limit
8. lastEarn is never updated (line 232 never reached)
9. Subsequent calls have even MORE epochs to iterate, making DoS permanent
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path is: getReward() → earned() → unbounded for loop with two binary searches per iteration. The loop count grows by 48 per day (86400/1800). After ~9 days: 432 iterations * ~35K gas/iter ≈ 15.1M gas > 15M block limit. Since lastEarn on line 232 only executes AFTER earned() completes, the feedback loop is: more epochs → more gas → OOG revert → lastEarn unchanged → even more epochs next time.

deployment_assumptions_checked: WEEK = 1800 is a compile-time constant in BlackTimeLibrary, not configurable
config_validation.deployment_evidence: BlackTimeLibrary.sol line 4: `uint256 internal constant WEEK = 1800;` — this is a Solidity constant, immutable after compilation. The hardhat config shows Avalanche deployment targets with gas: 8000000 for transactions.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack per se — it's a passive failure mode. Any user who does not claim bribe rewards for approximately 8-10 real-world days will find their rewards permanently locked. The scenario requires zero coordination or malicious action: vacation, forgotten wallet, inactive period, or simply not knowing rewards exist. Once the DoS threshold is crossed, there is no admin function to reset lastEarn, no pagination mechanism, and no alternative claim path. The rewards accumulate in the Bribe contract forever.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL severity. Any user inactive for ~9 days permanently loses all accumulated and future bribe rewards for that token. This affects both manual voters and potentially AVM-managed tokens (though AVM has separate issues). Given that the protocol targets Avalanche with a 15M block gas limit, and WEEK=1800 means 48 epochs/day, the window for safe claiming is unreasonably short. Users who hold veNFTs as long-term investments (which is the intended use case for vote-escrowed tokens) are especially vulnerable.
recommended_mitigations: Add an iteration cap to earned(): `uint256 maxEpochs = Math.min(numEpochs, 200);` and adjust lastEarn proportionally so users can claim in batches
Add a separate function to advance lastEarn without claiming rewards, allowing pagination of the catch-up
Increase WEEK to a more standard duration (e.g., 604800 for 7 days) which would require ~5.6 years to hit gas limits instead of ~9 days
Add an admin function that can reset lastEarn for affected users as an emergency recovery mechanism
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Bribe contract on Avalanche fork with WEEK=1800. 2. Create a veNFT and vote to deposit balance in Bribe. 3. Notify reward amounts for several epochs. 4. Advance block.timestamp by 10 days (480 epochs). 5. Call earned() and measure gas — expect >15M. 6. Call getReward() and observe OOG revert. 7. Advance by 1 more day and retry — observe even higher gas, confirming self-reinforcing DoS.
uncertainties: Exact gas per iteration depends on checkpoint array sizes (binary search depth); the ~35K estimate is conservative for small arrays but could be higher with many checkpoints
Avalanche C-chain block gas limit may vary (8M-15M depending on network conditions); lower limits make the issue trigger sooner
If the protocol has an off-chain keeper that claims very frequently, the issue may not manifest in practice — but the code defect still exists
additional_testing_needed: Gas profiling of earned() with varying checkpoint depths on Avalanche fork
Test with realistic bribe token counts (getReward loops over ALL tokens, compounding the issue)
Verify no external upgrade mechanism could patch lastEarn without redeployment
